free_text: (d,n) Reactions on Boron and Carbon
6 MeV Van de Graaff.
Two paragon-moderated BF3 counters.
The counter ratio method: One of these counters is
preferentially sensitive to low-energy neutrons
("slow counter") and the other is almost energy-
insensitive ("modified long counter"). The ratio of
the counting rate in the slow counter to that in the
modified long counter gives a measure of the number
of slow neutrons emitted at a particular bombarding
energy. As the energy is increased, sharp rises in
the "counter ratio" indicate the emission of
threshold neutrons to an energy level of the residual
nucleus. The increase of the counter ratio is made
even more pronounced by the fact that the efficiency
of the modified long counter, in its position behind
the slow counter, decreases rapidly for neutrons of
energy less than about 0.3 MeV.
BP
Two paragon-moderated BF3 counters.
A target of about 35-keV thickness
at a deuteron energy of 2 MeV was prepared by
evaporating boron metal, enriched to 96% B10, onto a
tantalum blank.
A target of normal boron metal, evaporated onto a
tungsten blank, was used in this experiment; it was
approximately 50-KeV thick at a deuteron energy of
2.5 MeV.
Carbon targets were prepared by
cracking methyl iodide, enriched to 68% C13, onto hot
tantalum blanks. The target used in this experiment
was approximately 15-keV thick at a deuteron energy
of 0.6 MeV and approximately 5-keV thick at 3 MeV.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
Table III, page 851 of J,PR,100,847,1955.
Cross
section data.
